name: Crispy Baked Egg Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1/4 cup water in a wok.
2. Add onions, ginger root, and garlic. Stir fry until onions are tender but not browned (about 5 minutes).
3. Add celery, mushrooms, cabbage, bamboo shoots, and water chestnuts. Continue to stir-fry until vegetables are tender (about 5-8 minutes).
4. Remove from heat and add soy sauce, rice wine, and honey. Toss well.
5. Place the mixture in a colander over a bowl and let it drain for at least 10 minutes.
6. Lay egg roll skins flat on a surface with one corner pointing away.
7. Spoon about 1/4 cup of drained filling into the center of each wrapper.
8. Brush the edges lightly with water, fold side corners to the center, covering the filling, bring the bottom corner to the center and tuck it under slightly.
9. Continue rolling into a cylinder and seal the top corner by moistening it slightly and pressing down.
10. The rolls may be frozen before cooking. Before baking, brush lightly with sesame oil.
11. Bake seam-side down on nonstick baking sheets at 400°F until golden and crispy (about 20 minutes). Serve immediately.
